steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content:
Factor $f(x)=x^3-16x$ by taking out $x$ and using the difference of squares.
$$x^3-16x=x(x^2-16)=x(x-4)(x+4)$$

steps.title: Cancel the common factor
steps.content:
Since $x>4$, $x-4\ne0$, so the common factor can be canceled without changing the value of the expression. This leaves $x$ times the remaining numerator factor over the remaining denominator factor.
$$\frac{x(x-4)(x+4)}{(x-4)(x+1)}=\frac{x(x+4)}{x+1}$$
The answer is D.
